title: Role-scoped executor routing (executor_kind_allowlist + claim_mode resolver)
goal: Implement the executor-routing decision surface that, given a thread and a candidate executor, decides whether the executor may claim/lease/respond on the thread per the thread's executor_kind_allowlist, claim_mode, and response_authority_scope. Provides a pure decision function `ExecutorRouter::decide(thread, executor) -> RouteDecision` reusable by every mailbox consumer (orchestrator, validator, automation, operator UI).
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/router.rs with `ExecutorRouter`, `RouteDecision`, and `RouteReason` types; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_router_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-176 thread types + MT-180 lease primitive merged so router can consume both.
implementation_notes: Per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.176] line 6153: 'Every actionable Role Mailbox thread SHOULD declare executor_kind_allowlist, claim_mode, lease_duration, takeover_policy, and response_authority_scope ... so local small models, cloud models, reviewers, validators, operators, and workflow automation do not race to answer the same thread.' This MT's `ExecutorRouter` is the pure decision layer. Signature: `pub fn decide(thread: &RoleMailboxThread, current_lease: Option<&RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1>, executor: &ExecutorIdentity) -> RouteDecision`. `ExecutorIdentity { executor_kind: ExecutorKind (LocalSmallModel|CloudModel|Reviewer|Validator|Operator|WorkflowAutomation), role_id: RoleId, session_id: Uuid v7, capabilities: Vec<Capability> }`. RouteDecision enum: `MayClaim { reason }`, `MayRespondInExistingLease { reason }`, `MayTakeover { predecessor_lease_id, reason }`, `MustWait { reason }`, `Denied { reason }`. Algorithm (pure, no I/O): (1) if executor.executor_kind not in thread.executor_kind_allowlist -> Denied("executor_kind not in allowlist"); (2) if thread.lifecycle_state is terminal (resolved/expired/archived) -> Denied("thread in terminal lifecycle state"); (3) if no current_lease and thread.claim_mode in [Exclusive, Handoff] -> MayClaim; (4) if current_lease.holder_session_id == executor.session_id -> MayRespondInExistingLease; (5) if current_lease.expires_at_utc < now -> MayClaim (stale lease); (6) if thread.takeover_policy permits -> MayTakeover; (7) else MustWait. response_authority_scope check applied per role_id (e.g., MicroTaskCompletionScope requires holder to also hold the linked MT executor lease — forward-declared, MT-189 wires the cross-check). All reasons are typed enums + a free-form String. Tests: exhaustive matrix of (claim_mode × current_lease present × executor_kind match × takeover_policy) — 32+ combinations; assert no panic on any input; golden-fixture covering each RouteDecision variant.
risk_if_missed: Without role-scoped routing, parallel executors race to claim or respond, the lease primitive (MT-180) loses its decision surface, and the cluster X 'one lawful ownership layer' degrades to mutex-by-database which is harder to audit and reason about.
validator_focus: Router is pure (no I/O, no clock except `now: SystemTime` injected as a parameter for testability); decision matrix is exhaustive; every Denied/MustWait has a typed reason validators can act on.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Normalized handoff expires_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision before bundle hashing.

note: MT-177 targeted repository lifecycle round trip passed.

execution_proof.summary: ExecutorRouter (src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/router.rs) extended with three additional gates: (0) empty-allowlist deny, (1a) ResponseAuthorityScope::OperatorOnly pre-lease deny for non-Operator executors, (5a) ClaimMode::Open multi-holder respond path with foreign active lease, and (6) full-policy match arm covering TakeoverPolicy::AlwaysWithReason + OperatorOnly+Operator (MayTakeover), TakeoverPolicy::OnLeaseExpiry / Never / OperatorOnly+non-Operator (MustWait). Added integration test file at the contract-named path src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_router_tests.rs with 22 pure-Rust adversarial assertions covering: (1) allowlist gate — wrong-kind / with-foreign-lease / empty allowlist / all-kinds permits / duplicate entries no-panic / conflicting allowlist determinism; (2) resolver — claim_mode deterministic per (Exclusive|Handoff|Open) / holder session match / Never policy MustWait / AlwaysWithReason policy MayTakeover / OperatorOnly + Operator MayTakeover + non-Operator MustWait / OnLeaseExpiry unexpired MustWait / ResponseAuthorityScope OperatorOnly denies non-Operator and permits Operator; (3) adversarial — terminal state denies all (Resolved/Expired/Archived) / clock boundary at expiry MayClaim / clock before expiry MustWait / serde round-trip preserves decision / ClaimMode::Open with foreign lease permits respond; (4) full 5-axis exhaustive matrix (claim_mode × takeover_policy × response_authority_scope × executor_kind × has_lease = 576 combinations) with no-panic + determinism check; (5) RouteDecision serde round-trip all variants + ExecutorKind snake_case wire shape. Registered [[test]] entry in src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml with required-features=['test-utils']. cargo test passes 22/22 integration + 8/8 router lib unit (no regression to adjacent role_mailbox_lease/repo/v1 tests).

focused_router_integration.note: 22 router integration tests passed in 0.01s wall clock after 7m 03s compile. No #[ignore]-gated tests; router is pure-Rust.

adjacent_no_regression.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_router_tests --test role_mailbox_lease_tests --test role_mailbox_v1_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
adjacent_no_regression.note: router 22 passed; lease 11 passed / 20 ignored; v1 9 passed / 2 ignored; repo 7 passed / 11 ignored. Total 49 passing, 33 ignored, 0 failed.

lib_unit_router.note: 8 in-router unit tests passed (denied_when_executor_kind_not_in_allowlist, may_claim_when_no_active_lease, must_wait_when_lease_held_by_other_no_takeover, may_respond_in_existing_lease_when_session_matches, may_claim_when_lease_expired, may_takeover_with_permissive_policy, denied_when_thread_terminal, exhaustive_matrix_does_not_panic). 1077 lib tests filtered out (focused run); router enhancements did not regress in-module assertions.

red_team_minimum_controls_addressed.now_injected_no_implicit_clock: Router decide(now: DateTime<Utc>) signature preserved; tests inject now at expiry boundary (mt_181_expired_lease_with_clock_at_expiry_boundary_returns_may_claim) and before expiry (mt_181_active_lease_with_clock_before_expiry_returns_must_wait) to prove no implicit clock dependency.
red_team_minimum_controls_addressed.exhaustive_combinatorial_sweep: mt_181_exhaustive_matrix_no_panic_5_axis covers 3 claim_modes × 4 takeover_policies × 4 response_authority_scopes × 6 executor_kinds × 2 has_lease = 576 combinations with no-panic + variant-determinism assertions.
red_team_minimum_controls_addressed.every_variant_non_empty_reason: mt_181_every_decision_variant_has_non_empty_reason positively exercises MayClaim / MayRespondInExistingLease / MayTakeover / MustWait / Denied with assert_reason_non_empty helper proving each variant carries a typed RouteReason and (for Denied) a non-empty detail.

minimum_controls: Router accepts `now: SystemTime` parameter (no implicit SystemTime::now()) so tests are deterministic.
Property-based test (proptest) generates arbitrary thread + lease + executor combos and asserts no panic.
Every RouteDecision variant has at least one positive test asserting the reason text is non-empty.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ExecutorRouter::decide is pure (no implicit clock), exhaustive (576-combination matrix sweep), and every RouteDecision variant carries a typed RouteReason. 22/22 integration + 8/8 lib unit tests green. Empty-allowlist, OperatorOnly, terminal-state, clock-boundary edges all covered. Path-shape drift (role_mailbox_v1/ vs role_mailbox/) is the only paperwork note; technique correctness is solid.
